rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical (or photoelectrochemical) polyfluoroalkylative cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with sodium fluoroalkanesulfinates to give 3-polyfluoroalkyl-4-pyrrolin-2-ones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (2a, Eox = 1.20 V vs Ag/AgCl) generates CF3 radical with loss of SO2; a second anodic oxidation converts the cyclized C-radical B into cation C; cathode reduces protons to H2. Under photoelectrochemical conditions, Mes-Acr+ is anodically oxidized to Mes-Acr•, whose excited state (Ered* = 1.78 V) oxidizes 2a and intermediate B.

key_intermediates.name: intermediate A: alpha-amido carbon radical after CF3 addition to acryloyl moiety
intermediate B: ring-closed C-centered radical after intramolecular addition onto enaminic C=C
intermediate C: cationic pyrrolinone after anodic oxidation of B

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (+ SO2); CF3• regioselectively adds to the acryloyl alkene of the 3-aza-1,5-diene giving C-radical A, which undergoes 5-exo intramolecular addition onto the enaminic C=C to give cyclized radical B; B is oxidized at the anode (or by Mes-Acr•*) to cation C, which loses a proton to furnish the 4-pyrrolin-2-one; cathode reduces H+ to 1/2 H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down (0% yield of 3a), supporting radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3a; DPE-CF3 adduct 4 isolated in 86%, confirming CF3• generation

probe_reagents.name: CCl4
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product, consistent with radical pathway

notes: Electricity on/off experiments show reaction stops without current, arguing against a long radical chain. CV shows 2a (1.20 V) is oxidized preferentially over 1a (1.81 V). H2 evolution at cathode confirmed by gas detection. Photoelectrochemical variant uses Mes-Acr+/Mes-Acr• as homogeneous mediator (excited-state Ered = 1.78 V) for sensitive substrates; this is a parallel mechanism rather than an alternative for the standard reaction.
